zotero.abstractNote: Heteroscedastic regression, where the predictive uncertainty depends on the input, is a fundamental task in machine learning. While deep neural networks (DNNs) are powerful function approximators, they are notoriously difficult to calibrate for uncertainty estimation. Bayesian neural networks (BNNs) offer a principled framework for uncertainty quantification, but they are often computationally expensive or perform poorly in practice. In this work, we propose a simple and effective approach for Bayesian heteroscedastic regression with DNNs. We leverage the Laplace approximation to estimate the posterior distribution of the weights, and we show that it can be efficiently applied to heteroscedastic models. We demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on a wide range of regression benchmarks, while being significantly more computationally efficient than existing Bayesian methods.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2023)
